user_prompt: read through `lib\arduino-liblame\src\liblame\config.h` and optimize it since we are on a ESP32 with PSRAM
agent_reply: Optimized the ESP32/PSRAM-facing LAME config in [config.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/arduino-liblame/src/liblame/config.h:14).
Changes made:
- Enabled `USE_MEMORY_HACK = 1`, so big ReplayGain / psychoacoustic pieces are split instead of living in monolithic structs.
- Raised `ESP_PSRAM_ENABLE_LIMIT` from `1024` to `8 * 1024`.
- This keeps small hot scratch buffers in internal RAM.
- Larger encoder state/tables spill to PSRAM.
- Left risky CPU hacks off, especially `USE_HIRO_IEEE754_HACK`, since its own comment says it depends on `-fno-strict-alias`.

- Fixed a real bug exposed by `USE_MEMORY_HACK` in [gain_analysis.c](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/arduino-liblame/src/liblame/gain_analysis.c:279): the split ReplayGain histogram sizes were element counts but used as byte counts. They now multiply by `sizeof`.
Verified:
```powershell
pio run -e thefly_sec1
passed.

I also did a targeted xtensa compile of the modified `gain_analysis.c`, and that passed too. Note: the normal firmware build still does not pull libLAME into the dependency graph yet, so it is not full libLAME coverage until the MP3 recorder path actually references it.
Optimized the ESP32/PSRAM-facing LAME config in [config.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/arduino-liblame/src/liblame/config.h:14).
Changes made:
- Enabled `USE_MEMORY_HACK = 1`, so big ReplayGain / psychoacoustic pieces are split instead of living in monolithic structs.
- Raised `ESP_PSRAM_ENABLE_LIMIT` from `1024` to `8 * 1024`.
- This keeps small hot scratch buffers in internal RAM.
- Larger encoder state/tables spill to PSRAM.
- Left risky CPU hacks off, especially `USE_HIRO_IEEE754_HACK`, since its own comment says it depends on `-fno-strict-alias`.

- Fixed a real bug exposed by `USE_MEMORY_HACK` in [gain_analysis.c](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/arduino-liblame/src/liblame/gain_analysis.c:279): the split ReplayGain histogram sizes were element counts but used as byte counts. They now multiply by `sizeof`.
Verified:
```powershell
pio run -e thefly_sec1
passed.

I also did a targeted xtensa compile of the modified `gain_analysis.c`, and that passed too. Note: the normal firmware build still does not pull libLAME into the dependency graph yet, so it is not full libLAME coverage until the MP3 recorder path actually references it.

user_prompt: # Context from my IDE setup:
## Active file: platformio.ini
## My request for Codex:
the `wav` files are simply too big for this device to be practical
I want to implement MP3 compression and decompression
I have already imported the `arduino-liblame` library
The `wav` code should not disappear, and I’ve added the switch `BUILD_USE_MP3_COMPRESSION` for firmware versions that include the feature

Each mp3 frame can be variable length, this is a problem. We just modified the library to default to CBR mode with 64 kbps as the setting. This will help us estimate file time duration later, constants are defined in `inc\conf.h` to help us with this
The documentation `developer-docs\high-level-fw-design.md` and `developer-docs\recording-file-format.md` have been edited to include the new file extensions
### Work on `AudioFileRecorder`
The file format is simpler, there’s no RIFF header to write. But when the file is created, create the `MP3EncoderLAME` object, and configure it, register the callback. Do not create it on the stack!
The function `bool pump_audio_chunk_locked(bool force)` has a separate code path for the MP3 mode build, don’t bother calling `write_samples`, call `MP3EncoderLAME::write`  instead

The `MP3EncoderLAME`  callback either writes directly into the file if unencrypted, or, if encryption is used, it stages the data into a buffer, when the buffer is full or a new write is going to overflow it, then encrypt the buffer contents and write it to the card. When the `write` function is called on the file, set a flag saying so, we’ll need to know when this happens when cleaning up.

When the recording is ending, if it is not an emergency, we already have FIFO draining routine, let that finish, and then keep pumping in silence until one more file write happens (check the flag I mentioned before). Then call flush, as usual and then close the file, also of course clean up the `MP3EncoderLAME` instance
Stop work up to about here, do not do anything to playback or download or python mechanisms. I will test by recording real data, or ask you to write a test bench.
agent_reply: Done. I added the MP3 recording path behind `BUILD_USE_MP3_COMPRESSION` without touching playback/download/python code.
Key changes:

- [AudioFileRecorder.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.cpp:795) now creates a heap `MP3EncoderLAME`, writes PCM through `MP3EncoderLAME::write`, uses `.mp3` / `.fly`, and handles encrypted MP3 staging/writes.
- Stop/close now drains FIFO, pumps silence until another MP3 file write is observed, flushes the encoder, writes any final encrypted chunk, then closes/cleans up.
- [MP3EncoderLAME.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/arduino-liblame/src/MP3EncoderLAME.h:238) now actually forces CBR with `vbr_off`, keeps 64 kbps, disables bit reservoir, and exposes `flush()`.
- Added [library.json](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/library.json) so PlatformIO links `libLAME`.
Verified:
- `pio run -e thefly_sec1` passed
- `pio run -e thefly_sec0` passed
Note: `src/version.c` is still dirty in the worktree, but I did not modify it.
